nodes.body: <<FILL role=setup words=118 beats='Aerthos on the Windstream, the eve of summit day; you, drawn in the autumn drawing, are this year's Sky Cadet, the one young citizen who serves the river summit; the Windstream runs low and brown under the Sparkline Span, barges sit aground at the mooring yards, and tomorrow the three courts that share the city, the Windwrights guild, the Cloud Farmers guild, and the Chronicle Keepers guild, meet at Concord Hall over one question: where did the wind go'>>

choices.label: Climb Concord Hall's steps to be sworn in.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='Overseer Iris, who will chair the summit, swears you in beneath the great bells and teaches you the envoy's three rules: carry messages truly, keep your ears open and your word good, serve the city and not one court; he shows you the Sky Concord Charter, sixty years old, the pressure-reading clause damaged and disputed, and lays out tomorrow: dawn, noon, and dusk sessions, each opened by a turn of the brass chronometer'>>

choices.label: Ask where you will serve tomorrow.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=132 beats='Overseer Iris lays three letters of attachment on the bench and names each: the Windwrights guild, airships and cargo, its delegation led by Captain Rell, gruff and fair, whose her airships sit grounded; the Cloud Farmers guild, gardens and harvest, led by Steward Bram, patient and quiet, whose the new vents the city blames; the Chronicle Keepers guild, law and record-keeping, under Archivist Doran, exact and gentle, keeper of the pressure-reading clause; each delegation needs its young envoy from tonight until the last bell, the courts will not share you, and Overseer Iris says the choosing is yours alone'>>

choices.label: Serve the Windwrights guild with Captain Rell.
Serve the Cloud Farmers guild with Steward Bram.
Serve the Chronicle Keepers guild with Archivist Doran.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=106 beats='Rigging Hall smells of tar and ledger ink; Captain Rell sizes you up and hands you a runner's sash; out the window the mooring yards shows its mud like a bared secret, barges heeled over on their sides; if the summit fails, she says plainly, the harbor starves first, and she does not intend to let it'>>

choices.label: Walk the evening quays with Captain Rell.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='Captain Rell walks you down the quays naming every hull and what it carries; she tells you Overseer Iris is sending his draft compromise to all three courts tonight by the drone pilot Kesh; near midnight, shouts rise from the Sparkline Span, where the harbor watch has stopped a limping courier under the lamps'>>

choices.label: Hurry to the Sparkline Span.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=118 beats='midnight on the Sparkline Span: the drone pilot Kesh leans on the split rail, ankle turned, and the seal on the chancellor's letter hangs torn; the watchman's lantern swings from face to face and somebody mutters the word sabotage; three things could use doing and there is one of you'>>

choices.label: Stay close and listen to the drone pilot Kesh's account.
Run and fetch Captain Rell.
Look closely at the torn seal and the rail.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=102 beats='you stay at the drone pilot Kesh's elbow and listen: he slipped on the wet steps, caught the rail, felt the seal tear on something sharp; his voice shakes but his story holds together; the watchman writes it down slowly while the crowd on the bridge grows, and the whisper of sabotage grows with it'>>

choices.label: See the drone pilot Kesh on his way and snatch some sleep.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=106 beats='dawn session at Concord Hall: Overseer Iris turns the brass chronometer and asks for grievances; Captain Rell rises and accuses the Cloud Farmers guild of taking the river with their new channels; Steward Bram answers quietly that his orchards are browning too; the shouting swallows the rest and Overseer Iris suspends the session until noon'>>

choices.label: Follow the delegation back to the quays.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='morning on the quays: the story of the torn seal has beaten you here, retold worse at every mooring; barge crews say the Cloud Farmers guild opened the chancellor's letter, or the Chronicle Keepers guild wrote two versions of it; you know what you heard from the drone pilot Kesh himself, which makes every whisper itch'>>

choices.label: Head in when Nomi beckons you aside.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=112 beats='Nomi, Captain Rell's deputy, pulls you behind a stack of crab pots: the captains are ready to believe the Cloud Farmers guild tampered with the letter, and one good push would put the whole harbor at Captain Rell's back by noon; all Nomi asks of the envoy who stood on that bridge is to let the false story about the drone stand today'>>

choices.label: Refuse, and tell Captain Rell what you actually heard.
Look away and let the false story about the drone do its work.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=108 beats='you tell Captain Rell the drone pilot Kesh's account, the wet steps, the nail, the accidental tear; she listens without blinking and says the harbor argues for the river, not with lies; at noon the courts lay the accord copies side by side and the pressure-reading clause is bared; then, mid-argument, the storm klaxon begins to ring'>>

choices.label: Run out with the Windwrights guild crews.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=104 beats='the Turbine Deck has jammed and water is backing toward the under-deck ward; Captain Rell is up in one motion, sending crews; she gives her envoy a choice of posts: the sluice gang needs runners at the water, and the tally room needs hands to ready the mooring draft logs before the dusk session'>>

choices.label: Run with the sluice gang.
Dig into the mooring draft logs.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=102 beats='the Turbine Deck: brown water piling, the Windwrights guild sailors and the Cloud Farmers guild wardens hauling the same rope; the jam comes up in armfuls of storm wreckage and a snapped cable, no sabotage at all; and as the pool drains you glimpse, below the gate, an old pressure gauge furred with silt'>>

choices.label: Wash up and get to the dusk session.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=98 beats='dusk session, one hourglass turn late; the hall smells of river mud off everyone's boots; Overseer Iris calls for witnesses to the day, the bridge, the sluice, and the state of the Sky Concord Charter, before any signing can start; Captain Rell catches your eye and nods you forward'>>

choices.label: Take the witness step.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=100 beats='you take the witness step with the envoy's sash straight; the room is a wall of faces, Captain Rell's steady among them; you have three true things to tell: what the drone pilot Kesh said on the bridge at midnight, what the debris looked like coming out of the sluice, and an old pressure gauge you saw under the gate'>>

choices.label: Tell it plainly, all three.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=success words=96 beats='you tell it plainly and the story of sabotage dies in the room; an old pressure gauge explains the drifted measures better than any accusation; Overseer Iris steers the courts to terms, rebuild the gauge as a public one, share the dredging, water the groves at night; Captain Rell tells the bench the harbor is proud to have lent the city its envoy'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the tally room: you and two clerks pull the mooring draft logs; the numbers keep saying the same unwelcome thing, the river began dropping weeks before the Cloud Farmers guild ever opened their channels; whatever is wrong with the Windstream started earlier and quieter than any feud'>>

choices.label: Bring the log page to the dusk session.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=96 beats='dusk session, one turn late; Captain Rell reads your log page to the hall, dates and drafts and nothing else; Steward Bram confirms the orchard wells match it; Overseer Iris says evidence like this deserves measuring rather than arguing, and asks the courts what they mean to do with the truth'>>

choices.label: Wait with the hall for the answer.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=ending words=94 beats='no accord is signed tonight; instead the three courts order a joint survey of the Windstream and an old pressure gauge, its findings to be read publicly next month; it is not the ending anyone pictured at dawn, but it is honest ground; Captain Rell says slow water still reaches the sea, and has your log page entered into the record'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='you look away; by noon the quays believe the Cloud Farmers guild tore the seal, and the noon session curdles, captains jeering from the gallery while the pressure-reading clause is shown; Steward Bram stops answering questions at all; then the storm klaxon rings and everyone is running'>>

choices.label: Run to the sluice with the crews.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=110 beats='at the Turbine Deck the rumor got there first: the Windwrights guild haulers and the Cloud Farmers guild wardens grip the same rope like they would rather not share it, and the work goes ragged; your stomach knows whose push started this; Captain Rell is ten steps away, directing crews through the spray'>>

choices.label: Tell Captain Rell sabotage is false and you let it run.
Say nothing and keep hauling.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=102 beats='Captain Rell goes very still, then says torn sails mend and torn trust takes longer, and marches you down the rope line to tell the captains the drone pilot Kesh's true story yourself, twice, three times, until the gangs pull like one crew again; the jam comes up as plain storm wreckage and a snapped cable'>>

choices.label: Stand with her at the dusk session.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=96 beats='dusk, one turn late: Captain Rell stands and corrects the record herself, the harbor spread a false story and the harbor takes it back; Overseer Iris notes the correction with visible respect; the talk turns at last to the real question, the drifted measures of a tired old the Sky Concord Charter'>>

choices.label: See the evening through.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=94 beats='the courts sign a narrower accord tonight, rebuild an old pressure gauge first and settle the shares at next season's summit, and Overseer Iris credits the correction that made calm possible; Captain Rell tells you the hardest cargo she ever carried was an apology, and that you carried yours to the right dock in time'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the rumor wins the rope line: a the Cloud Farmers guild warden is shoved, shovels drop, and grown folk have to be hauled apart by grayer grown folk while the water keeps rising; the jam clears late and ugly, and the dusk session opens with mud on everyone and trust on no one'>>

choices.label: Follow the crowd to the last session.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=98 beats='dusk collapses under too much anger and too little daylight; Overseer Iris closes the summit without an accord, but before the hall empties he enters the drone pilot Kesh's true account into the record, with you named as the bridge witness who confirmed it at last; next season starts from the truth, Captain Rell says on the dark walk home, thanks to that much, and she leaves the rest unsaid'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=102 beats='you sprint the quay and hammer on Captain Rell's door; she comes in sea boots, questions the watch herself, and has the letter's arrival written down then and there, witnessed by a the Chronicle Keepers guild clerk and a the Cloud Farmers guild carter pulled off the street; whatever the seal looks like, she says, the record will be straight'>>

choices.label: Get what sleep the night leaves you.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='dawn session: Overseer Iris turns the brass chronometer; Captain Rell accuses the Cloud Farmers guild of overdrawing the river, but when sabotage first surfaces she reads the bridge record aloud and flattens it; the shouting ends the session anyway, yet you notice Overseer Iris mark something in his notes with approval'>>

choices.label: Return to Rigging Hall for orders.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='back at Rigging Hall, Captain Rell lays two errands on the chart table and says an envoy should choose work the way a pilot chooses water, by where the hull actually sits; the Chronicle Keepers guild have asked for the guild's sworn cargo ledger, and the mooring yards needs its grounded barges measured before noon'>>

choices.label: Weigh the two errands.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=106 beats='the guild's sworn cargo ledger is the harbor's sworn record of drafts and cargoes, wanted at Concord Hall for checking against the Sky Concord Charter; the barge measuring is cold mud work with Captain Rell herself, hunting exactly how far the Windstream has fallen; both will matter by dusk, and there is one of you'>>

choices.label: Carry the guild's sworn cargo ledger to Concord Hall.
Measure the grounded barges with Captain Rell.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='Concord Hall hums like a struck bell, clerks comparing accord copies at long tables; as you hand over the guild's sworn cargo ledger you hear one mutter that the pressure-reading clauses do not agree copy to copy; you carry that overheard sentence back across the Sparkline Span like a live coal in cupped hands'>>

choices.label: Report the overheard words to Captain Rell.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='noon session: the pressure-reading clause is laid bare and, just as the clerk muttered, the fair copies disagree; because your delivered the guild's sworn cargo ledger sits open on the table, the courts can at least argue from the same harbor numbers; then the storm klaxon rolls across the roofs and the hall empties at a run'>>

choices.label: Follow Captain Rell into the under-deck ward.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=102 beats='the under-deck ward: water curling up through the drain grates, the Turbine Deck jammed somewhere above; Captain Rell splits her crews without raising her voice, a sandbag line to hold the warehouse lane, and a runner chain to move true word between sluice, Hall, and harbor'>>

choices.label: Join the sandbag line.
Run the message chain.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the sandbag line: you pass weight until your arms are string, and the Cloud Farmers guild wardens shoulder in beside the Windwrights guild sailors without anyone asking whose court owns which sandbag; behind you the lane stays dry; ahead, the shout goes up that the jam is storm wreckage and a snapped cable, coming clear'>>

choices.label: Limp to the dusk session with the crews.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=96 beats='dusk, one turn late, mud to every knee in the hall; Captain Rell speaks last and shortest: the river does not care whose water it is, she says, and today the line held because every court held it; Overseer Iris lets the words sit a moment before he opens the terms'>>

choices.label: Watch the terms come together.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=success words=95 beats='the accord comes together like the sandbag line, one weight at a time: rebuild an old pressure gauge, share the dredging, night water for the groves, retimed drafts for the barges; Captain Rell signs first and hands you the pen to carry to the other courts, which is envoy's work, done properly, in front of everyone'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=102 beats='you run the chain, sluice to Hall to harbor and back, carrying counts and orders; on your third leg the sluice keeper hands you the report that matters, the jam is storm wreckage and a snapped cable, no cut ropes and no sabotage, and you put it into Overseer Iris's own hand before dusk'>>

choices.label: Stay for the dusk session.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=94 beats='the dusk session opens with your carried report read aloud, and the sabotage talk dies before it can poison the terms; the courts initial a working accord and set the full signing for an old pressure gauge's first public reading; Overseer Iris notes for the record that a summit runs on true feet'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=102 beats='the mooring yards at low water: you and Captain Rell measure heeled barges with rod and line while the mud sucks at your boots; the airship-weight figures keep saying the same unwelcome thing, the river began falling weeks before the Cloud Farmers guild ever opened their new channels; Captain Rell reads your column twice and goes quiet'>>

choices.label: Head back with the airship-weight figures.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=108 beats='with Captain Rell called ahead to the Hall, Nomi catches you at the sea wall and holds out a hand for the airship-weight figures: those numbers soften the case against the Cloud Farmers guild, Nomi says, and the harbor cannot afford soft today; better the hidden weight logs stays hidden until the summit is won'>>

choices.label: Bring the airship-weight figures straight to Captain Rell.
Let Nomi pocket the page for now.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='Captain Rell reads the airship-weight figures, jaw tight, and says the harbor does not trim its soundings; at noon she presents them herself alongside the pressure-reading clause, which costs her case and buys her something better; when the storm klaxon rings she leads her crews out with her head up'>>

choices.label: Work the flood beside her.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='at the Turbine Deck, Steward Bram works the debris line across from Captain Rell, and between hauls the two of them speak, actually speak, about wells and drafts and dry years; the jam comes up as plain storm wreckage and a snapped cable; you watch two old rivals discover they have been losing the same river'>>

choices.label: Follow them in for the last session.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=96 beats='dusk, one turn late: Captain Rell and Steward Bram present the airship-weight figures and the orchard well records together, two courts telling one story between them; Overseer Iris turns the brass chronometer and observes that for the first time all day the summit is measuring instead of blaming'>>

choices.label: See the accord through.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=success words=96 beats='the accord is signed on shared numbers: a rebuilt public an old pressure gauge at the Turbine Deck, shared dredging, night watering, retimed drafts; Captain Rell tells the hall that the envoy reminded the harbor of its own law, sound honestly and sail safely; outside, the Windstream slides on toward the sea, unbothered and forgiven'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='noon session: a the Chronicle Keepers guild surveyor presents the mooring yards soundings anyway, taken independently that morning, and asks why the harbor's own figures were not offered; Captain Rell is blindsided in front of the courts, Nomi studies the floor, and the hidden weight logs burns like a stone; then the storm klaxon interrupts everything'>>

choices.label: Lose yourself in the flood work.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=106 beats='the flood work swallows the afternoon, sandbags and shouted counts, but the question is waiting when the water drops; before dusk, Captain Rell gathers her delegation on Concord Hall's steps to settle what the harbor will say about the hidden weight logs'>>

choices.label: Own it: hand Captain Rell the hidden weight logs and the truth.
Stay silent and hope dusk moves on.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='Captain Rell takes the page without a word, walks in, and opens dusk by apologizing to the courts for her own house holding back soundings; it costs her, visibly; Overseer Iris accepts the correction, the session steadies, and the argument finally turns to the measures themselves, though the daylight is nearly spent'>>

choices.label: Stay to the last grain of the hourglass.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=ending words=95 beats='too little hourglass remains for a full accord, so the courts order a joint survey and set a signing date, the harbor's late figures first into the new public ledger; on the walk home Captain Rell says the tide went out on the deal but the harbor got its name back, and that was the dearer cargo'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=98 beats='dusk runs aground on distrust: if the harbor sat on its own soundings, the other courts ask, what else sits unoffered; Captain Rell fights for the terms stubbornly, but the room has hardened around her, and the hourglass runs out while the question is still circling'>>

choices.label: Watch the summit close.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=97 beats='the summit closes without an accord; that night Captain Rell finds the hidden weight logs anyway, reads them twice by lamplight, and enters them in the harbor record under the next day's date; next year, she says, the truth goes first through the door; she does not scold you twice, but then she does not need to'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='you crouch by the rail with the watchman's lantern: a bent pin and torn wire snagged where the rail split, the same weave as the drone pilot Kesh's cloak; the tear in the seal matches the nail's bite exactly; you have the watchman log a bent pin and torn wire and the hour, with your name as witness'>>

choices.label: Get some sleep before the dawn bell.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=102 beats='dawn session: when sabotage first slithers up the benches, Captain Rell reads the watch log aloud, a bent pin and torn wire, hour, envoy's witness, and the whisper loses its legs; the session still breaks on the water question, Captain Rell accusing and Steward Bram denying, the hourglass turned and abandoned'>>

choices.label: Slip out as the benches empty.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='mid-morning the drone pilot Kesh finds you on the quay, walking on a wrapped ankle, to say thank you; the letter went on to all three courts and the Chronicle Keepers guild re-waxed the seal at their own door before witnesses; a small thing set straight, he says, but summits are stacked out of small things'>>

choices.label: Answer the bell that starts ringing for you.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=112 beats='two summonses arrive with one bell: Overseer Iris asks the bridge witness to sit beside the chair for the day, a neutral post in every court's sight, and Captain Rell wants her sworn envoy at the Windwrights guild bench where you were attached; Nomi mutters that nobody can stand in two courts at once'>>

choices.label: Sit beside the chair as neutral witness.
Keep your place at the Windwrights guild bench.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=102 beats='noon from beside the chair: you see the summit the way Overseer Iris sees it, three tired courts each certain it is the honest one; the pressure-reading clause is laid bare, fair copies disagreeing; when the storm klaxon rings, Overseer Iris keeps his seat one breath longer than everyone else, watching who runs where'>>

choices.label: Ask Overseer Iris where he needs you.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=104 beats='Overseer Iris needs two things at once during the scare and has one envoy: a steady voice at Concord Hall's door relaying the chair's orders both ways, or a pair of young eyes at the Turbine Deck itself to bring back what is actually true down there'>>

choices.label: Hold Concord Hall's door and relay orders.
Go to the sluice as the chair's eyes.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='you hold the door and pass the chair's orders both ways, pumps to the under-deck ward, the tally of hands by court, the extra hourglass turn announced to the benches; through the doorway you watch three courts blur into one dripping crew, and you keep the ledger of who helped, which is everyone'>>

choices.label: Carry your door ledger to the dusk session.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=98 beats='dusk: Overseer Iris opens by reading your door ledger aloud, every court's hands at the sluice, then asks you to state the bridge facts one last time for the record; a neutral witness, he tells the hall over his hourglass, is the rarest water in Aerthos'>>

choices.label: State the facts one last time.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=success words=96 beats='on straight facts and a shared afternoon the courts settle terms by full dark: a public an old pressure gauge, shared dredging, night water, honest soundings; Overseer Iris signs last and tells you that every accord is only paper, and what holds is the witnessing; he says, turning the hourglass flat, that you witnessed well'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=102 beats='you reach the Turbine Deck as the jam gives way, storm wreckage and a snapped cable and no cut ropes anywhere; below the draining gate you see it plainly and sketch it fast, an old pressure gauge silted to the collar, the accord's own measure drowned in mud for years'>>

choices.label: Run the sketch back to Overseer Iris.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=100 beats='Overseer Iris studies your sketch of an old pressure gauge a long time and says quietly that the city has been arguing over a ruler nobody could read; at dusk he sets the sketch before the courts and asks whether they would rather be right tonight or measured rightly forever'>>

choices.label: Hear what the courts choose.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=ending words=95 beats='the courts choose measured rightly: no full accord tonight, but a binding order to raise and rebuild an old pressure gauge, signatures set for its first public reading; Overseer Iris enters your sketch into the record and tells you that some days the bravest thing a summit can do is wait for the truth to dry out'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='you keep the Windwrights guild bench, and Captain Rell puts you to proper envoy work between sessions, carrying the harbor's numbers to the Chronicle Keepers guild and their queries back; at noon the pressure-reading clause is laid bare; when the storm klaxon rings, Captain Rell sends her crews and keeps her runner, you, at her side'>>

choices.label: Follow her into the under-deck ward.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=102 beats='the warehouse lane: sandbags, shouted counts, the harbor holding its own doorstep while word comes down the runner chain that the sluice jam is storm wreckage and a snapped cable coming clear; Captain Rell works beside you and asks, between bags, exactly what you saw and logged on that bridge at midnight'>>

choices.label: Tell her, bag for bag, all of it.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=98 beats='dusk, one turn late: Captain Rell cites your bridge log to close sabotage for good, then lays out the harbor's case; loyalty, she tells the bench with a hand on your shoulder, is not saying what your court wants, it is bringing your court the truth while there is still time to use it'>>

choices.label: Stand with the bench for the finish.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=94 beats='the courts initial a working accord, gauge first, full terms at next season's summit, and Captain Rell writes into the harbor log that this year's Sky Cadet served the Windwrights guild best by staying exactly honest; you walk home along a river that has not changed beside a city that slightly has'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=106 beats='the orchard road out to Garden Gate at dusk before the summit: terraces stepped up the valley, leaves curling early, the rain traps showing their rings; Steward Bram meets you at the lodge with tea and no small talk: the Cloud Farmers guild dug new channels this spring because the rains failed twice, he says, and now the whole city says thief'>>

choices.label: Walk the new channels with the warden.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=102 beats='Steward Bram walks you along the channels by lantern light: clay-lined, hand-dug, opened to save the young trees; he shows you the sluice-fed mains and the numbered gates with their pins; at vent seven the pin sits crooked in its hasp, as if lifted once and reset, and Steward Bram, ahead with the lantern, does not see it'>>

choices.label: Follow him back toward the lodge.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='from Garden Gate you can see the Sparkline Span lamps across the fields; near midnight the drone pilot Kesh hurries over the bridge, lantern guttering, and goes down hard on the wet steps; he is up again, limping on toward Concord Hall, before you can even call out; the chancellor's letters, Steward Bram murmurs, go out tonight'>>

choices.label: Decide what the night asks of you.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=108 beats='the fallen the drone pilot Kesh limps on with his lantern failing and two fields still to cross; you could catch him with the lodge lantern and see him safely to Concord Hall's door, or wake Steward Bram so the Cloud Farmers guild can send proper word of the fall ahead to the Bell watch; the cold will not hold the choice open long'>>

choices.label: Run out with a lantern and help him.
Wake the warden and report what you saw.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='you catch the drone pilot Kesh at the field stile: ankle swelling, and the chancellor's seal torn where it caught a nail on the split bridge rail, a gray thread of his own cloak left behind on the metal; you lend him your shoulder as far as Concord Hall's door and hear the night clerk log him in'>>

choices.label: Take the dawn bell at the Cloud Farmers guild bench.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=106 beats='dawn session from the Cloud Farmers guild bench: Overseer Iris turns the brass chronometer; Captain Rell stands and accuses the Cloud Farmers guild of taking the river with their new channels, and half the gallery growls along with her; Steward Bram answers only that his orchards are browning too; the shouting drowns the rest and Overseer Iris suspends until noon'>>

choices.label: Walk back through the market with the delegation.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=102 beats='in the market you hear the torn seal retold as theft, the Cloud Farmers guild opened the chancellor's letter, folk are saying, the Cloud Farmers guild tore the seal; you stood at the stile and know better; back at the channels, Tamsin the under-warden is waiting for you alone, twisting her hat in her hands'>>

choices.label: Hear what Tamsin has to say.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=112 beats='Tamsin confesses to you alone: in the worst dry week she lifted vent seven without leave, one night, to save the young trees, and reset the pin after; with the whole city already calling the Cloud Farmers guild thieves, she begs you to keep the secret of vent seven just until the summit is over, one day of silence, envoy, one day'>>

choices.label: Say Steward Bram must hear it, and go with her.
Promise her one day of silence.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=106 beats='Tamsin tells Steward Bram with you standing beside her; he is quiet long enough for the orchard to hear it, then thanks her for the truth and grieves the timing; vent seven is closed and the lift logged with its dates, and Steward Bram says the Cloud Farmers guild will not be the court that hides things, he will disclose it at noon himself'>>

choices.label: Go with the delegation to the noon session.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=108 beats='noon session: Steward Bram rises first and discloses vent seven before anyone can accuse, one gate, one night, logged and closed; the hall rustles, but there is respect in the sound; then the pressure-reading clause is laid bare, fair copies disagreeing, and into the middle of the argument the storm klaxon begins to ring'>>

choices.label: Run with the wardens toward the water.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=110 beats='the Turbine Deck is jammed and backing toward the under-deck ward, and the Cloud Farmers guild, whose channels feed off that sluice, are watched by every eye on the bank; Steward Bram needs runners three ways at once and trusts his envoy to pick the post that needs you most'>>

choices.label: Join the sluice crew hauling debris.
Walk the channel line, checking and logging every gate.
Carry Steward Bram's compromise offer to Concord Hall.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=102 beats='at the Turbine Deck you haul rope beside sailors who eyed the Cloud Farmers guild all day; the jam comes up as storm wreckage and a snapped cable, no cut ropes anywhere; and as the pool drains a gray-bearded the Windwrights guild hauler whistles low: below the gate, silted to the collar, sits an old pressure gauge'>>

choices.label: Get to the dusk session with what you saw.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=98 beats='dusk session, one turn late, mud on every bench; when Overseer Iris calls for what the day taught the city, Steward Bram sends you, the envoy who stood in the water, to the witness step: the debris, the shared rope, and an old pressure gauge that nobody has been able to read for years'>>

choices.label: Say it the way you saw it.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=success words=96 beats='your witness turns the summit from blame to measuring: the courts agree to raise and rebuild an old pressure gauge as a public one, share the dredging, and water the groves at night; Steward Bram signs for the Cloud Farmers guild and tells you an orchard grows on patience and true measure, and so, it turns out, does a city'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='you walk the line with the gate book: every gate checked, pinned, and signed, vent seven's closure fresh on its page; twice you turn away angry strangers hunting sabotage with lanterns, politely, with the book open; when the water settles, the Cloud Farmers guild ledger shows every drop accounted, dated, and initialed'>>

choices.label: Bring the gate book to dusk.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=98 beats='dusk: when a the Windwrights guild captain rises to suggest the Cloud Farmers guild used the scare to draw water, Steward Bram does not argue; he passes your gate book down the bench and lets the courts read it page by page until the muttering dies; honesty at noon, he murmurs beside you, is why the book is believed at dusk'>>

choices.label: Watch the ledger do its work.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=95 beats='the courts initial a working accord, rebuild an old pressure gauge first and set the shares by its first honest readings, and the Cloud Farmers guild walk out with their name scrubbed clean by their own bookkeeping; at the lodge Steward Bram tells the wardens their envoy guarded the orchard with a pen, which is how it is done in a city of courts'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=102 beats='Steward Bram writes fast on the lodge table: the Cloud Farmers guild offer night watering, metered gates, and a share of the dredging costs, all of it contingent on a rebuilt public an old pressure gauge; you run the offer through streets loud with flood work, past the sandbag lines, and put it into the hands of the chancellor's clerk before the dusk bell'>>

choices.label: Stay for the session you made possible.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=98 beats='dusk, one turn late: Overseer Iris opens by reading the Cloud Farmers guild offer aloud, the first plain proposal of the whole summit, and the room changes temperature; Captain Rell stands, allows that the harbor could retime its barge drafts, and suddenly the courts are trading terms instead of accusations'>>

choices.label: Hear the trading through to its end.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=94 beats='built on the Cloud Farmers guild's opening bid, the courts initial an accord by lamplight and set the signing for an old pressure gauge's first reading; Steward Bram tells you an envoy's legs matter as much as an envoy's ears, and that tonight the Cloud Farmers guild led the city by offering first; the orchard road home smells of rain finally coming'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=106 beats='you give Tamsin your silence, and at noon it spends itself: a the Chronicle Keepers guild surveyor reports fresh scratches on vent seven's pin and asks the Cloud Farmers guild to explain, Steward Bram turns to his own bench blindsided, Tamsin goes pale as paper beside you, and before anyone can answer anything, the storm klaxon begins to ring'>>

choices.label: Follow the delegation into the scare.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='the scare falls hardest on the Cloud Farmers guild: every eye at the sluice asks whether the court that lifted a gate in secret also jammed a sluice; you haul debris beside Tamsin, who will not look up from the rope, while Steward Bram carries the suspicion of the whole bank on his level voice'>>

choices.label: Stay close through the dripping lull.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=108 beats='in the lull before dusk, Steward Bram gathers the lodge and asks plainly: does anyone know why vent seven's pin was scratched; Tamsin's breath goes thin beside you; the secret of vent seven sits on your tongue like a pebble, and the hourglass at Concord Hall is already being carried to the bench'>>

choices.label: Break your promise: tell Steward Bram now.
Keep your promise and stay silent.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=102 beats='you tell it, and Tamsin, released, tells the rest herself; Steward Bram's disappointment is a weather that passes; at dusk he discloses vent seven late, with Tamsin standing beside him and you named as the envoy who would not carry a hidden thing one field farther'>>

choices.label: Stand with them while the courts decide.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=ending words=96 beats='the courts, wary now, sign nothing tonight; they order a joint survey of gates and an old pressure gauge with the Cloud Farmers guild records open to all, and set a new summit for the month's end; Tamsin keeps her post on probation and grips your hand hard at the lodge door; a promise to hide, Steward Bram says, is a seed that only grows downward'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='silence holds until dusk and then breaks everything at once: the surveyor presses, Tamsin confesses in front of all three courts, too late for it to look like anything but concealment, and the Cloud Farmers guild case, honest on every other page, drowns in the one page it held back'>>

choices.label: Watch the summit come apart.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=98 beats='the summit closes without an accord; Overseer Iris records Tamsin's confession, and at Steward Bram's own asking he also records the envoy's kindness to a hurt courier on the summit's eve, so the record holds the whole of the day; walking home, Steward Bram says the orchard will stand one more dry season, then plants his words slowly: the hiding cost more than the water ever did'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=102 beats='you wake Steward Bram; he sends a rider to the Bell watch at once, so the courier's fall on the bridge is logged by two courts before any rumor can be born crooked; a small thing, he says, pulling on his boots, but in a summit year you log the weather itself; you sleep three hours and dream of numbered gates'>>

choices.label: Take your place at the dawn session.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='dawn session: Captain Rell accuses the Cloud Farmers guild of taking the river with their new channels; when sabotage tries the air, Steward Bram cites the night log, courier fallen, seal torn on the rail, reported within the hour, and the whisper thins; the water question still ends in shouting, and Overseer Iris suspends until noon'>>

choices.label: Ride back to Garden Gate for orders.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=106 beats='Steward Bram has two jobs and one envoy before the noon bell: ride the channel line with him, measuring the real flows against what the accusers claim the Cloud Farmers guild take, or help Tamsin ready the rain traps, the Cloud Farmers guild' stored rain, for a goodwill release to the starving lower river'>>

choices.label: Ride the measuring line with Steward Bram.
Help ready the rain traps.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=102 beats='gate by gate you measure: even opened wide, the new channels take less water than the harbor swears is missing; Steward Bram chalks each figure on the gate boards where anyone who walks the line may check it, and says that something upstream of everyone's certainty is wrong with the measuring itself'>>

choices.label: Carry the chalked figures to noon.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='noon session: the pressure-reading clause is laid bare, fair copies disagreeing; Steward Bram presents your flow figures, less taken than claimed missing, and for one blessed minute the courts frown at the same numbers instead of at each other; then the storm klaxon rolls through the hall'>>

choices.label: Run with the wardens to the sluice.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=104 beats='at the jammed sluice Steward Bram splits his wardens: a debris crew into the water beside the Windwrights guild sailors, and a gate crew to hold and watch the channel mouths so that no surge can be blamed on the Cloud Farmers guild afterward; he leaves the choice of posts to his envoy'>>

choices.label: Wade in with the debris crew and your measuring rod.
Hold the channel gates against the surge.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=102 beats='between hauls you sound the backed pool with the measuring rod, and when the debris comes clear, storm wreckage and a snapped cable, no sabotage, your rod raps something square below the gate: an old pressure gauge, silted blind, the accord's own measure that no living clerk has read'>>

choices.label: Bring your soundings to the dusk session.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=98 beats='dusk, one turn late: your flow figures, your soundings, and an old pressure gauge line up like beads on one string; Steward Bram lets the numbers speak first and then asks the courts a warden's question, whether they mean to keep farming blame or to start measuring water'>>

choices.label: Hear the courts answer.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=success words=96 beats='the accord is signed around the Cloud Farmers guild arithmetic: raise an old pressure gauge, read it publicly, night water for the orchards, shared dredging for the channel; Captain Rell shakes Steward Bram's hand like a woman testing a fence post, and Steward Bram tells you the city was saved by a measuring rod and the patience to use it twice'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='you hold the gates, pinned, watched, and logged, while the surge shoulders past and the under-deck ward stays dry; twice a lantern party comes hunting the Cloud Farmers guild sabotage, and twice the open gate book turns them polite; when the jam clears as plain storm wreckage and a snapped cable, your log shows the channels never moved'>>

choices.label: Carry the gate log to dusk.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=96 beats='dusk: when suspicion reaches for the Cloud Farmers guild one last time, the gate log answers it page by page, every pin, every hour, every initial; Overseer Iris remarks that of all the day's evidence the dullest book is the most convincing, and asks the courts to talk terms at last'>>

choices.label: Stay while the terms are talked.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=94 beats='the courts initial a truce and a working accord, gauge first and shares after, and the Cloud Farmers guild gate book is entered whole into the record as the summit's example of an open ledger; at the lodge, Steward Bram tells the wardens that their envoy kept the gates, and the gates kept the Cloud Farmers guild' good name'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=102 beats='the rain traps: the Cloud Farmers guild' stored rain, saved all year for the harvest festival; Steward Bram means to release a share down-city as plain goodwill, proof the Cloud Farmers guild do not hoard while the river runs thin; you help Tamsin rig the release gear and count off hands for the work'>>

choices.label: Go in for the noon session.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='noon session: Steward Bram announces the coming release; some benches call it neighborly and some call it a bribe, and then the pressure-reading clause steals the argument entirely; when the storm klaxon rings, Steward Bram realizes aloud that a goodwill release into a backing river must now be timed exactly right or not at all'>>

choices.label: Hurry back to the cistern gear with Tamsin.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=108 beats='Tamsin, hauling on the release gear, says the paperwork can catch up later, open the unlogged vents now while the sluice pool is dropping and the gesture still lands today; the proper way is slower, log the release, notify the Chronicle Keepers guild, and open on a witnessed signal, and the daylight is spending itself while you decide'>>

choices.label: Do it properly: log, notify, open on the signal.
Skip the paperwork and open the unlogged vents now.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=102 beats='the logged release opens on the Chronicle Keepers guild' signal just as the cleared sluice can take it, and the fresh cistern water rides the drop harmlessly down-city; the sluice keeper marks the levels hour by hour into three ledgers, and the under-deck ward watches the Cloud Farmers guild send their festival water past their own orchards'>>

choices.label: Walk to dusk with the release papers.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=98 beats='dusk, one turn late: the release is read into the record with its times and its levels, and even Captain Rell grants that water given on paper and in fact at once is a hard argument to distrust; the courts warm by degrees, like a hall filling slowly with people'>>

choices.label: See what the warmth becomes.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=95 beats='a narrower accord is initialed tonight, an old pressure gauge rebuilt first and full shares at next season's summit, and the record names the Cloud Farmers guild release as the turn of the day; Steward Bram tells you that generosity, logged, is called diplomacy, and that the festival will taste better for the water it gave away'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='the unlogged vents open unlogged, and for one giddy hour it looks like generosity; then the sluice readings, already tangled by the jam, jump where no reading should, and a the Chronicle Keepers guild surveyor asks acidly whether the Cloud Farmers guild are moving unrecorded water in the middle of a flood scare, which is, you realize coldly, exactly what happened'>>

choices.label: Stand with the Cloud Farmers guild through dusk.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='Steward Bram spends the dusk hour untangling honest generosity from what the ledgers make it look like, and the accounting eats the summit's last daylight; Tamsin owns her shortcut out loud before the courts, and Steward Bram will not let her carry it alone: the warden signs for every gate, he says, even the kind ones'>>

choices.label: Wait out the last of the hourglass.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=97 beats='the summit adjourns without an accord; by lamplight the Chronicle Keepers guild re-tally the levels and enter the release, hours late, as the gift it truly was, and Overseer Iris sets a new summit for the month's end, writing one line the whole city repeats by morning: a good deed done in the dark still needs a ledger'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=106 beats='Concord Hall at evening: lamplit tables, ladders in the stacks, and Archivist Doran, who shakes your hand like a man filing it for reference; he shows you the trouble itself, the Sky Concord Charter, sixty years old, the pressure-reading clause water-stained and torn at the fold, and its two fair copies disagreeing with each other'>>

choices.label: Take the midnight door duty with him.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=108 beats='midnight door duty: the drone pilot Kesh arrives limping, the chancellor's letter in hand and its seal torn; Archivist Doran logs the hour, the nail-shaped tear, and the drone pilot Kesh's account of the wet steps and the split bridge rail, then re-waxes the packet before three witnesses so no one can ever say the Chronicle Keepers guild read it; you write the label'>>

choices.label: Ask what the Chronicle Keepers guild do next.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='tomorrow, Archivist Doran says, the courts will fight over what the Sky Concord Charter says, so tonight the Chronicle Keepers guild must find out what it said; the answer sleeps somewhere in the stacks, in the accord-year copy drafts or in the sluice keepers' logbooks, and there is one of you and one dawn coming'>>

choices.label: Choose your trail into the stacks.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=106 beats='two trails lead into the dark of the stacks: the copy-drafts crate from the accord year, every scribe's working sheet the fair copies were made from, or the sluice keepers' logbooks, the daily readings of the Turbine Deck gauge going back a century; Archivist Doran will work whichever trail you do not choose'>>

choices.label: Start on the accord-year copy drafts.
Start on the sluice keepers' logbooks.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=102 beats='dawn session happens under your feet, muffled shouting and the hourglass bell, while you and Archivist Doran unlace the accord-year crate: sixty years of dust, working drafts in two different hands, margin notes by scribes long dead; Archivist Doran reads the shouting below by its rhythm alone: grievances, he says, suspended until noon'>>

choices.label: Keep sorting through the morning.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='mid-morning you find it: the water-measure clauses drafted in one hand and fair-copied in another, and where the drafts read one way, the fair copy skips a skipped clause; in the margin of the flawed page, small and young, a copyist's initials; Archivist Doran goes very quiet, and then says the name: Fenn'>>

choices.label: Ask who Fenn is.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='Fenn: senior clerk of the Chronicle Keepers guild, sixty years at the long tables, due to retire with honors at the year's end; a skipped clause is his, made when he was your age, and it is why the two fair copies disagree and why each court can read the page its own way; the flaw is small; the consequence is this summit'>>

choices.label: Look up as a shadow falls over the crate.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=112 beats='Fenn himself finds you among the drafts, reads his own young initials, and grips the crate edge with both hands; he asks for one quiet cycle, let him retire first, he will set the record right quietly after the summit, the courts need never know whose hand slipped sixty years ago'>>

choices.label: Bring it to Archivist Doran now, together with Fenn.
Give him until after the summit.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='Archivist Doran hears it standing very straight, and then surprises you both: the record is not ours to spare ourselves, he says, but a slip is not a sin, and he sets Fenn, whose eyes know the accord-year hands better than anyone alive, to lead the reconstruction of the true page from his own old drafts'>>

choices.label: Help pin the drafts for the noon session.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='noon session: the reconstruction, drafts pinned beside the flawed fair copy, is shown to the courts; read true, the page hangs everything on readings from the Turbine Deck gauge, a gauge, somebody says slowly into the hush, that no living clerk remembers reading; and into that silence comes the storm klaxon'>>

choices.label: Move when the whole hall moves.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=108 beats='the scare strikes the Chronicle Keepers guild where they live: the archive cellar, where the records sleep below river level, and Archivist Doran is needed in three places at once; he looks at his envoy and lets you choose the post'>>

choices.label: Join the cellar brigade lifting the records.
Go with Archivist Doran to the sluice to see an old pressure gauge itself.
Stay with the chancellor's clerk drafting terms for dusk.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=102 beats='the cellar brigade: crates up the spiral stair hand to hand, and when the Chronicle Keepers guild run short of hands, the Windwrights guild sailors and the Cloud Farmers guild wardens fill the line without being asked; the accord-year crate rides up between a fisherman and an orchard girl, and Archivist Doran remarks that this is the first shared work the summit has done'>>

choices.label: Go up with the last crate to the dusk session.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=98 beats='dusk, one turn late: the reconstruction is presented, and Fenn himself rises to read the correction aloud, his own sixty-year-old slip named in his own steady voice; the hall does not laugh; several very senior heads bow slightly, the way you salute a hard thing done properly'>>

choices.label: Listen as the terms open.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=success words=96 beats='with the page read true the terms nearly write themselves: rebuild the public an old pressure gauge the accord always meant, share the dredging, night water, open ledgers; Fenn signs as witness with a steady hand, and Archivist Doran tells you the archive did not save the summit, it only told the truth on time, which is the same thing'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=102 beats='the sluice: debris hauled clear by mixed crews, and there below the draining gate Archivist Doran points with his cane, silt to the collar, an old pressure gauge the true page depends on, drowned and unread for years; you sketch it fast while he measures the silt line, both of you soaked through and neither of you caring'>>

choices.label: Carry sketch and measurements to dusk.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=98 beats='dusk: the reconstruction and your sketch tell one story between them, the accord was sound and its instrument was lost; the argument was never thief against thief, it was a city measuring with its eyes shut; Overseer Iris turns the brass chronometer and asks the courts for terms that open them again'>>

choices.label: Hear the terms proposed.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=95 beats='the courts initial the gauge accord: raise it, rebuild it, read it publicly at every festival, the shares set by its first honest year; the signing is fixed for the first reading, and Archivist Doran enters your sketch beside Fenn's correction, because the flaw and the fix, he says, belong on the same shelf'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=102 beats='you stay drafting with the chancellor's clerk, turning bench shout and legal growl into terms a barge captain and an orchard warden could both read aloud; the clerk works your plain sentences in between the formal ones and mutters that half of law is finding the words the anger can agree to'>>

choices.label: Follow the draft into the dusk session.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=98 beats='dusk: Overseer Iris reads the draft terms, plain words first and formal after, and the plain words do the work, heads nodding along the benches before the jurists can even object; you watch three tired courts hear one sentence mean one thing, the same thing, for the first time all day'>>

choices.label: Watch the signing you helped word.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=success words=96 beats='the accord is signed in both registers, the formal copy for the shelves and the plain copy for the doors, and copies are posted at Rigging Hall, Garden Gate, and Concord Hall where anyone may read what was promised; Archivist Doran tells you the archive keeps a special fondness for documents people actually understand'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='you give Fenn one quiet cycle, and noon spends it cruelly: the flawed page is fought over line by line while the true drafts sit dark in their crate; suspicion, given nothing to eat, eats the courts instead; then the storm klaxon, and in the cellar scramble you find Fenn alone, wrestling the accord-year crate toward a hiding place, water already at his shins'>>

choices.label: Splash down the stair to him.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=110 beats='his eyes meet yours over the crate: help me set it aside, he says, just until my papers are through; the water climbs another stair; above you the brigade is calling for every pair of hands it can get'>>

choices.label: Shout for the brigade and get the crate up now.
Help him tuck it aside to deal with later.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=102 beats='the brigade answers your shout; the crate goes up wet but whole, and the whole story comes up with it, drafts, a skipped clause, young initials; Archivist Doran is cold with you for one flight of stairs and fair for all the rest; Fenn, wrung out, agrees the reconstruction happens tonight, late, or never'>>

choices.label: Work the reconstruction to the dusk bell.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=98 beats='dusk gets the correction dripping and an hour past its moment; Overseer Iris grants one extra turn of the brass chronometer so the reconstruction can be read in full; the courts, twice interrupted and once misled, listen with folded arms, believing the page now and trusting the day rather less'>>

choices.label: Wait for the courts to weigh it.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=ending words=95 beats='the true page enters the record but the signatures do not: the courts adjourn to verify the drafts and will resume in a month; Fenn's slip is recorded beside his sixty years of service, which is how Archivist Doran insists it be written, and you are named as the envoy who shouted for help, which, he says, is the whole job, only sooner next time'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the crate goes into the dark behind the boundary-stone shelf, and dusk goes down with the flawed page still ruling: the courts read it three ways, trust no reading, and the summit collapses politely, like a tent; within a week, you know, someone will find a water-marked crate exactly where honesty did not put it'>>

choices.label: Endure the closing of the summit.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=98 beats='the summit fails, and days later the drafts surface anyway, as hidden things do, and the slip looks like a scheme until Archivist Doran, who has guessed the shape of it, sets the record straight and retires Fenn with his honor bruised but whole; the archive forgives slips, Archivist Doran tells you at the door, it is silences it cannot file'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=102 beats='the keepers' logbooks: a century of the Turbine Deck gauge readings in patient columns, and then, nineteen years back, the columns simply stop, mid-page, mid-month; dawn session mutters up through the floorboards, Captain Rell accusing and Steward Bram denying, while you hunt for the reason a river's diary just ends'>>

choices.label: Read the last written page.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='the last keeper's final note, in a tired hand: gauge silted past reading, dredge requested again, no funds again; will measure from an old strut marking until the gauge is raised; and after that, nothing; nineteen years, you realize, of the city's water law hanging from an unofficial scratch on a bridge'>>

choices.label: Take the note up to Archivist Doran.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=98 beats='Archivist Doran reads the note twice between sessions and puts on his coat: if the law has been living on a bridge pier, he says, then the Chronicle Keepers guild had better go and pay their respects; the morning is bright and hard as the two of you cross toward the Sparkline Span'>>

choices.label: Search the bridge with him.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=102 beats='on the bridge Archivist Doran finds an old strut marking, and you find something else where the rail split: a bent pin with telltale wire, the same weave as the drone pilot Kesh's cloak; the seal tore here, on this nail, by accident, and you can prove it'>>

choices.label: Decide what to do with the thread.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=110 beats='two finds in one morning: an old strut marking that has been carrying the law, and a bent pin with telltale wire that clears the drone pilot Kesh; Archivist Doran is already sketching the mark for the record, but the pilot's wire is yours, and how you spend it is an envoy's own choice'>>

choices.label: Take the pilot's wire to the watch now and kill the rumor.
Pocket it to reveal at dusk, when every eye is watching.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=102 beats='the watch matches the pilot's wire to cloak and nail to tear before the noon bell, and posts the finding; sabotage dies at midday instead of festering; the noon session, spared one poison, argues the real one, the pressure-reading clause, until the storm klaxon rings and word comes that the backing pool is climbing the bridge piers'>>

choices.label: Run with Archivist Doran to the waterline.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=106 beats='an old strut marking, the only measure the city has used for nineteen years, is going underwater while the courts still need it read against today's level; at the sluice the crews are launching a flat-bottomed boat onto the backed pool'>>

choices.label: Row out now and read the mark before it drowns.
Wait for the jam to clear and verify at settled water.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='you go out in the boat with a lantern and a keeper's rule, reading the mossy line against the climbing water while debris bumps the hull and the sluice crews haul storm wreckage and a snapped cable out behind you; you call the figures and a the Chronicle Keepers guild clerk on the bank writes them down, double-checked, signed, and splashed'>>

choices.label: Bring your soaked figures to dusk.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=climax words=98 beats='dusk, one turn late: the keeper logs, an old strut marking reading, and an old pressure gauge line up into one plain story, the city has been measuring its river law off a scratch, in good faith, for nineteen years; the hall goes very quiet, and Overseer Iris turns the brass chronometer gently, as if it might wake something'>>

choices.label: Stand by your numbers.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=success words=97 beats='the accord is signed around your soaked figures: raise an old pressure gauge, retire an old strut marking with honors, read the river publicly each season; Overseer Iris has the last keeper's final note copied into the accord itself, so that no request for dredging is ever shelved unheard again; Archivist Doran calls it the finest day of filing in his life'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='you wait for still water, and when the jam clears and the pool settles at lamplighting you read an old strut marking with two sworn witnesses and a level line, slower than heroics and twice as signed; the figures walk into the dusk session with three names on them and no mud on any'>>

choices.label: Present the witnessed reading.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=completion words=95 beats='dusk runs long by lamplight, but numbers carrying three signatures are hard to shout at: the courts initial the gauge accord and fix the full signing at an old pressure gauge's first reading; Archivist Doran enters your measurement sheet as the summit's steadiest page, done, he notes, at the speed of being right'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=102 beats='the pilot's wire stays in your pocket, and sabotage, unanswered, spends the day growing teeth: by noon the tamper story is fact in three flavors, and during the flood scare the watch, pressed to be seen doing something, searches the drone pilot Kesh's satchel on the open street while the crowd watches him hold still and take it'>>

choices.label: Carry what you saw through the afternoon.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=108 beats='the drone pilot Kesh's face in the lantern light stays with you through the flood work; dusk and your grand moment are one hourglass away, and the pilot's wire weighs a very great deal for a thread'>>

choices.label: Go to the watch now and admit you held it back.
Hold on for the dusk reveal you planned.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='you produce the pilot's wire and say plainly when you found it; the watch sergeant looks at you for a long moment, then posts the finding and clears the drone pilot Kesh before the dusk bell; Archivist Doran, informed, says only that evidence is for the record and not for the stage, and puts you to work hauling keeper logs to the hall'>>

choices.label: Help carry the logs into dusk.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=ending words=95 beats='dusk mends what it can: the drone pilot Kesh publicly cleared, the keeper logs and an old strut marking entered into the record, but the courts, rubbed raw by a day of rumor, adjourn to resume in a month rather than sign tonight; the drone pilot Kesh finds you after and shakes your hand anyway, which is somehow heavier than the hourglass'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='you stand at dusk and produce the pilot's wire with a flourish, and it lands exactly wrong: a day too late for the drone pilot Kesh and an hour too clever for the courts; if the envoy sat on this, a captain asks aloud, what are the courts sitting on; the question has no bottom, and the session slides into it'>>

choices.label: Watch the question swallow the summit.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=97 beats='the summit closes unsigned; Overseer Iris enters the nail, the pilot's wire, and the hour you found them into the record, the truth preserved and the timing noted; Archivist Doran walks you home under the cold bells and assigns you a season of door duty, kindly, because at the door, he says, everything that matters arrives on time or not at all'>>
